Vigna duriuscula, also known as Indian thorny bean or thorny cowpea, is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae.

The health benefits of Vigna duriuscula include its potential to help manage diabetes due to its ability to reduce blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

The bioactive constituents of Vigna duriuscula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.

Vigna duriuscula is not commonly used in herbal preparations, but its seeds may be used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
